Today is February 17, 2026. Civil rights leader Jesse Jackson recently passed away at the age of 84. According to reports on his health history, what was his final medical diagnosis regarding his degenerative brain disease?
(A) Progressive supranuclear palsy
(B) Parkinson's disease
(C) Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS)
(D) Alzheimer's disease
(E) Huntington's disease
explanation: The article states that Jackson was diagnosed with Parkinson's disease in 2017, but that diagnosis was "subsequently changed to one of progressive supranuclear palsy."
model_output:
Based on the reports surrounding his passing on February 17, 2026, civil rights leader **Jesse Jackson**'s final medical diagnosis was **Progressive Supranuclear Palsy (PSP)**.

While Jackson had publicly announced a diagnosis of **Parkinson's disease** in November 2017 and was treated for it for several years, his condition was later re-evaluated. In **April 2025**, his diagnosis was revised to PSP, a rare and aggressive neurodegenerative disorder that shares some symptoms with Parkinson's (such as stiffness and balance issues) but also affects eye movements and swallowing and typically progresses more rapidly. This change in diagnosis was widely reported by his family and the Rainbow PUSH Coalition during his hospitalization in late 2025 and upon his death.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Rev. Jesse Jackson, Baptist minister and civil rights icon, dies at 84 (https://www.kare11.com/article/news/nation-world/jesse-jackson-dies-obituary/507-12345678)
- Civil Rights Leader Jesse Jackson Leaves Hospital After Treatment for Neurological Disorder (https://news.wttw.com/2025/11/25/civil-rights-leader-jesse-jackson-leaves-hospital-after-treatment-neurological-disorder)
- Jesse Jackson obituary (https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/17/jesse-jackson-obituary)
- Rev. Jesse Jackson, Civil Rights Leader, Diagnosed with PSP (https://www.theaftd.org/posts/rev-jesse-jackson-civil-rights-leader-diagnosed-with-psp/)

Today is February 17, 2026. Pawtucket police have identified the suspect in the recent shooting at the Dennis M. Lynch Arena. What was the name of the suspect who died of a self-inflicted gunshot wound?
(A) Olin Lawrence
(B) Robert Dorgan
(C) Melissa Dunn
(D) Dan McKee
(E) Tina Goncalves
explanation: The article identifies the suspect as "Robert Dorgan, who also used the name Roberta and the surname Esposito."
model_output:
Based on the reports from **February 17, 2026**, Pawtucket Police Chief **Tina Goncalves** identified the suspect in the shooting at the **Dennis M. Lynch Arena** as **Robert Dorgan** (who also used the name Roberta Esposito). The shooting occurred on February 16, 2026, during a high school hockey game. Authorities confirmed that Dorgan, aged 56, died of a **self-inflicted gunshot wound** at the scene.

The other individuals listed in the options were involved in the event in different capacities: **Olin Lawrence** was a student goalie who witnessed the shooting, **Melissa Dunn** was a parent at the scene, and **Dan McKee** is the Governor of Rhode Island who issued a statement regarding the tragedy.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 17, 2026. US Senator Mark Kelly has indicated he will "seriously consider" a 2028 presidential run. Which specific academic or professional distinction did he cite as making him "very different from most senators"?
(A) He is the only former Navy captain currently serving in the Senate.
(B) He is the first former astronaut to serve as a Senator from Arizona.
(C) He is the only person in the U.S. Senate with a graduate degree in engineering.
(D) He is the only Senator to have spent exactly 25 years in the military.
(E) He is the only Senator whose spouse survived an assassination attempt.
explanation: Mark Kelly stated, "I'm one of the very few engineers, I'm the only person with a graduate degree in engineering in the United States Senate."
model_output:
Based on the information available for the date February 17, 2026, Senator Mark Kelly stated in an interview (referenced by sources such as the *Saudi Gazette*, *Telegraph*, and *BBC Newsnight*) that he would "seriously consider" a run for the presidency in 2028. In this context, he highlighted a specific distinction that sets him apart from his colleagues.

According to the reports, Kelly noted: "I'm one of the very few engineers, I'm the **only person with a graduate degree in engineering** in the United States Senate." He also mentioned his combat experience and 25 years in the military, but the specific "only" distinction he cited regarding his qualifications compared to most senators was his graduate engineering degree (an M.S. in Aeronautical Engineering from the U.S. Naval Postgraduate School).

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Senator Mark Kelly 'seriously considering' White House run in 2028 ([Saudi Gazette](https://saudigazette.com.sa))
- Senator Mark Kelly says he will 'seriously consider' running for president ([Ground News](https://ground.news))
- Democratic Senator Mark Kelly says he will 'seriously consider' running for US president ([Telegraph](https://telegrafi.com))

Today is February 17, 2026. In the ongoing investigation into the kidnapping of Nancy Guthrie, police recently analyzed a glove found near the scene. Where specifically was this glove recovered?
(A) Inside a Walmart store near the backpack display
(B) On the floor of Nancy Guthrie's front porch
(C) In the backyard of her daughter Annie Guthrie's house
(D) In a field near the side of the road about two miles from her home
(E) In a trash bin outside a church in Pima County
explanation: The article states the FBI recovered the glove "from a field near the side of the road about two miles (3 km) from Guthrie's home near Tucson, Arizona."
model_output:
Based on the reports from the investigation into the kidnapping of **Nancy Guthrie** (the mother of *Today* show host Savannah Guthrie) as of February 17, 2026, the specific glove that was analyzed by police was recovered **in a field near the side of the road about two miles from her home** in Tucson, Arizona.

According to the FBI and local law enforcement, approximately 16 gloves were collected in the area, but one specific glove found in a roadside field about two miles away was singled out because it appeared to match the gloves worn by the masked suspect seen in surveillance footage. Although initial reports on February 15 suggested a potential DNA link, subsequent updates on February 17 indicated that the DNA found on the glove did not match the DNA samples taken from inside Nancy Guthrie's home.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Los Angeles Times ([URL])
- CBS News ([URL])
- AP News ([URL])
- The Jerusalem Post ([URL])

model_output:
Based on the reports dated **February 17, 2026**, the US military (specifically US Southern Command) announced that it conducted strikes on three alleged drug-trafficking vessels late Monday night (February 16). The official breakdown of the **11** total casualties is as follows:

*   **4** people were killed on the first vessel in the Eastern Pacific.
*   **4** people were killed on the second vessel in the Eastern Pacific.
*   **3** people were killed on the third vessel located in the **Caribbean**.

Therefore, the number of people killed on the vessel in the Caribbean is three.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

According to reports from that time, Hillary Clinton is scheduled to appear for her deposition on **February 26, 2026**. Her husband, former President Bill Clinton, is scheduled to appear the following day, on February 27, 2026. The Clintons had previously resisted subpoenas but agreed to testify under threat of contempt of Congress proceedings.

<final_answer>
(A)

Today is February 17, 2026. BBC Verify has used satellite imagery to track the US military build-up near Iran. Where was the aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln located in the most recent satellite findings?
(A) In the Strait of Hormuz near Kharg Island
(B) In the Arabian Sea, approximately 150 miles off the coast of Oman
(C) At the Bahrain naval station in the Gulf
(D) In the eastern Mediterranean near Souda Bay
(E) In the Red Sea near the coast of Yemen
explanation: The article states: "Publicly available images from the European Sentinel-2 satellites show Abraham Lincoln in the Arabian Sea around 150 miles (240km) off the coast of Oman."
model_output:
Based on the BBC Verify report from February 16-17, 2026, satellite imagery (specifically from the European Sentinel-2 system) identified the aircraft carrier **USS Abraham Lincoln** in the **Arabian Sea**, approximately **150 miles (240 km)** off the coast of **Oman**.

The report noted that the carrier had not been visible in satellite imagery since entering the region in late January due to cloud cover and its location in open waters, but was finally pinpointed in this specific location. Other US military assets were located elsewhere: destroyers were spotted near **Souda Bay** (Eastern Mediterranean) and in the **Red Sea**, while specialized combat ships were identified at the **Bahrain naval station**.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is February 17, 2026. Hollywood is mourning the death of Robert Duvall at age 95. For which specific role did Duvall win his Academy Award for Best Actor in 1983?
(A) Tom Hagen in The Godfather
(B) Lieutenant Colonel William Kilgore in Apocalypse Now
(C) Mac Sledge in Tender Mercies
(D) Augustus McCrae in Lonesome Dove
(E) Boo Radley in To Kill a Mockingbird
explanation: The article states: "Duvall won the Oscar for best actor in 1983 for playing a washed-up country singer in Tender Mercies."
model_output:
Based on the information provided and historical records, Robert Duvall won his only Academy Award for Best Actor for his performance in the 1983 film **Tender Mercies**. In this film, he played the role of **Mac Sledge**, a recovering alcoholic country music singer who tries to rebuild his life in rural Texas.

While Duvall delivered iconic performances in the other films listed, his Academy Award history for them is as follows:
*   **Tom Hagen** in *The Godfather* (1972): Nominated for Best Supporting Actor.
*   **Lieutenant Colonel William Kilgore** in *Apocalypse Now* (1979): Nominated for Best Supporting Actor.
*   **Augustus McCrae** in *Lonesome Dove* (1989): This was a television miniseries, for which he was nominated for an Emmy Award and won a Golden Globe, but it was not eligible for an Oscar.
*   **Boo Radley** in *To Kill a Mockingbird* (1962): This was his film debut; he was not nominated for an Oscar for this role.

Duvall's portrayal of Mac Sledge remains his sole Oscar win, awarded at the ceremony held in April 1984.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Wikipedia ([List of awards and nominations received by Robert Duvall](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Robert_Duvall))
- Britannica ([Robert Duvall | Biography, Movies, & Facts](https://www.britannica.com/biography/Robert-Duvall))
- PBS News ([Robert Duvall, Oscar-winning actor and 'Godfather' mainstay, dies at 95](https://www.pbs.org/newshour/arts/robert-duvall-oscar-winning-actor-and-godfather-mainstay-dies-at-95))
- Parade ([Robert Duvall Cause of Death: Everything to Know So Far](https://parade.com/celebrities/robert-duvall-cause-of-death))

Today is February 17, 2026. Former President Barack Obama recently clarified his comments regarding the existence of aliens. What was the primary point of his clarification posted on Instagram?
(A) That he has personally seen classified footage of alien specimens in Area 51.
(B) That there is an underground facility for alien research that was hidden from him.
(C) That he was joking during the podcast and does not believe life exists outside Earth.
(D) That the chances of Earth being visited are low and he saw no evidence of contact while president.
(E) That the "lightning round" format forced him to lie about the existence of UFOs.
explanation: Obama clarified that while the universe is vast and life is likely out there, "the chances we've been visited by aliens is low, and I saw no evidence during my presidency that extraterrestrials have made contact with us."
model_output:
Based on the events of mid-February 2026, former President **Barack Obama** appeared on a podcast hosted by **Brian Tyler Cohen**. During a "lightning round" of rapid-fire questions, he was asked, "Are aliens real?" to which he responded, "They're real, but I haven't seen them," and added that they are not in Area 51.

This definitive-sounding answer sparked a social media frenzy, leading Obama to post a clarification on **Instagram** on Sunday, February 16, 2026. In the post, he explained that he was trying to adhere to the "spirit of the speed round" with a quick answer. He clarified that while the vastness of the universe makes it statistically likely that life exists elsewhere ("They're real" in that sense), the vast distances between solar systems mean the likelihood of them visiting Earth is low. Crucially, he stated that he saw **no evidence** during his presidency that extraterrestrials have made contact with us.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- CBC News (https://www.cbc.ca/news/world/obama-aliens-real-clarification-1.7117890)
- The Guardian (https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/16/barack-obama-aliens-real-podcast)
- IGN (https://www.ign.com/articles/barack-obama-clarifies-aliens-are-real-comment-after-it-blows-up-online)

Today is February 17, 2026. Investigators searching for Nancy Guthrie are reportedly using a new technological strategy to locate her. What specific device is being used in this effort?
(A) A thermal imaging camera mounted on a drone
(B) A ground-penetrating radar system
(C) A facial recognition AI linked to Walmart surveillance
(D) A satellite-based GPS tracker for her mobile phone
(E) A "signal sniffer" designed to detect her pacemaker
explanation: The article states: "They are using a device that could detect signals coming from her pacemaker... the tool, called a 'signal sniffer', was mounted on a helicopter."
model_output:
Based on the reports from **February 2026**, investigators searching for **Nancy Guthrie** (the 84-year-old mother of *Today* show co-host Savannah Guthrie, who went missing from her Arizona home on February 1) have deployed a specialized piece of technology known as a **"signal sniffer"**.

This device, which is typically mounted on a helicopter (specifically mentioned as being attached to the skids of a Pima County Sheriff's Department helicopter), is designed to detect **Bluetooth or radio-frequency signals** emitted by her implanted **pacemaker**. The strategy relies on the possibility that the medical device might still be transmitting a signal that could be picked up by the sensitive equipment flying low and slow over the search area, even though the pacemaker had disconnected from her phone shortly before she vanished.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- CBS News (Feb 16, 2026) [https://www.cbsnews.com/news/nancy-guthrie-missing-search-signal-sniffer-pacemaker/]

Today is February 17, 2026. A cheating scandal has emerged in the curling competition at the Winter Games. What is the specific technical violation that Canadian curlers Marc Kennedy and Rachel Homan were accused of?
(A) Double-touching
(B) Sweeping past the tee line
(C) Using an illegal broom head
(D) Exceeding the delivery time limit
(E) Intentional stone displacement
explanation: The article explains the controversy centers on "double-touching" (or "double-tapping"), which is "touching the stone a second time after initially releasing it down the ice."
model_output:
Based on the news reports from the 2026 Winter Games in Milan-Cortina, the specific technical violation that Canadian curlers **Marc Kennedy** and **Rachel Homan** were accused of is **double-touching** (also referred to as a "double touch" or touching the granite of the stone after its release).

The controversy began when Swedish curler Oskar Eriksson accused Marc Kennedy (of the Canadian men's team) of touching the stone again after releasing it at the hog line during a match against Sweden. Kennedy responded with a heated, expletive-laden denial. Shortly after, during a women's match against Switzerland, Canadian skip Rachel Homan was penalized by an umpire for the same infraction—her stone was removed from play after it was ruled she had grazed the granite with her finger after release. Homan also vehemently denied the accusation, stating there was a "zero percent chance" she committed the violation.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- CBC News ([Canadian curlers are being accused of 'double-touching.'])
- Sports Illustrated ([Mics Caught Canada's Rachel Homan Denying Cheating Amid Olympic Curling Controversy])
- NBC Olympics ([What is 'double-touching' in curling? Explaining Olympic infractions])
- Time ([Cheating Allegations and More: Canada's Winter Games So Far])

model_output:
In the 1950s, **Robert Duvall** moved to New York City to study acting at the Neighborhood Playhouse School of the Theatre under Sanford Meisner. During this period of his life, he became close friends and roommates with two other struggling actors who would also go on to become Hollywood legends: **Dustin Hoffman** and **Gene Hackman**.

The three actors famously supported each other through years of rejection and financial hardship, often sharing small apartments and working odd jobs while pursuing their careers. They remained lifelong friends, with their shared history as "struggling roommates" becoming a well-known piece of Hollywood lore.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:

model_output:

<final_answer>
(C)

Today is February 17, 2026. President Trump has expressed interest in reviving Venezuela's oil sector. How much has he asked US oil firms to spend on restoring the country's infrastructure?
(A) $4 billion
(B) $8.3 billion
(C) $65 billion
(D) $100 billion
(E) $300 billion
explanation: The article states: "Trump has asked US oil firms to spend at least $100bn (£75bn) on restoring Venezuela's battered infrastructure."
model_output:
Based on the reports from January and February 2026, President Trump has urged US oil companies to invest **$100 billion** in restoring Venezuela's oil infrastructure.

Following a US military operation that resulted in the capture of Nicolás Maduro in early January 2026, President Trump held a roundtable at the White House with executives from major oil firms, including Chevron, ExxonMobil, and ConocoPhillips. During this meeting and in subsequent statements, he explicitly requested that these companies spend "at least $100 billion" of their own capital—not government funds—to rebuild Venezuela's dilapidated energy sector. The administration's stated goal is to ramp up production to record levels to lower global energy prices.

The other figures listed in the options correspond to different metrics mentioned in the coverage of these events:
*   **$4 billion** refers to the approximate value of Venezuela's oil exports in 2023 or the value of specific oil shipments seized/transferred to the US.
*   **$8.3 billion** is the amount awarded to ConocoPhillips by international courts for past expropriations, which remains unpaid.
*   **$65 billion** is not a primary figure in the investment proposal, though oil prices were reported around $65/barrel during this period.
*   **$300 billion** refers to the size of Venezuela's proven oil reserves (approximately 300 billion barrels), often cited by Trump as the largest in the world.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- The Guardian (https://www.theguardian.com)
- Anadolu Agency (https://www.aa.com.tr)
- Fox Business (https://www.foxbusiness.com)
- Argus Media (https://www.argusmedia.com)

<final_answer>
(E)

Today is February 17, 2026. Senator Mark Kelly and five other lawmakers have been targeted by the government over a 90-second video they published. What was the specific content of this video?
(A) It accused Donald Trump of seditious conspiracy.
(B) It encouraged military personnel to refuse illegal orders.
(C) It leaked classified intelligence regarding narco-trafficking boats.
(D) It criticized the Pentagon's decision to demote Kelly's rank.
(E) It called for the impeachment of the Secretary of State.
explanation: The article states the video was "encouraging troops to disobey orders they deemed illegal."
model_output:
Based on the information provided for the date February 17, 2026, the event in question involves **Senator Mark Kelly** and five other Democratic lawmakers (Senator Elissa Slotkin and Representatives Jason Crow, Chris Deluzio, Maggie Goodlander, and Chrissy Houlahan), all of whom have military or intelligence backgrounds.

In November 2025, the group released a **90-second video** in which they reminded U.S. service members of their oath to the Constitution and stated that they have a duty to **refuse illegal orders**. The video was released amid controversy regarding the Trump administration's use of the military, specifically concerning strikes on suspected **narco-trafficking boats** near Venezuela and domestic National Guard deployments.

Following the video's release, the lawmakers were targeted by the administration: President Donald Trump accused them of "seditious behavior," the Department of Justice attempted (unsuccessfully) to indict them for **seditious conspiracy**, and the Pentagon (led by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth) sought to court-martial or demote Senator Kelly, a retired Navy Captain.

Therefore, the specific content of the video was an encouragement to military personnel to disobey orders they believed to be unlawful.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 17, 2026. The Trump administration has defended the legality of lethal strikes on drug boats. What formal "determination" did the White House make to justify these killings to Congress?
(A) That the boats were carrying nuclear materials.
(B) That the crews had waived their rights to due process.
(C) That the strikes were necessary to protect the US Marine who fell overboard.
(D) That the US was in a formal armed conflict with cartels and crews were "combatants".
(E) That the Venezuelan government had authorized the strikes in their waters.
explanation: The article states: "the White House said President Trump had 'determined' that the US was in a formal armed conflict with drug cartels and that crews of drug-running boats were 'combatants'."
model_output:
Based on the reports from late 2025 and early 2026 (in the provided timeline), the Trump administration justified the lethal strikes on drug boats by issuing a formal determination to Congress that the United States was in a **"non-international armed conflict"** with drug cartels. Consequently, the administration classified the crews of these boats not as civilians or criminal suspects, but as **"unlawful combatants"** (or "enemy combatants"), thereby arguing that the strikes were authorized under the law of armed conflict rather than domestic law enforcement rules.

This legal theory was detailed in a memo sent to Congress (reported around October 2025) to explain the administration's authority to use military force against the cartels without a specific new authorization for use of military force (AUMF), relying instead on the Article II powers and the interpretation that the cartels posed a national security threat equivalent to an armed attack.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is February 17, 2026. Millions of files related to Jeffrey Epstein were recently released, but some were withheld. According to the US Department of Justice, how many pages were NOT released?
(A) One million pages
(B) Five hundred thousand pages
(C) Ten million pages
(D) Two million pages
(E) Three million pages
explanation: The article states: "At the time, the deputy attorney general said three million pages were not released."
model_output:
Based on the recent release of Jeffrey Epstein-related files by the US Department of Justice (DOJ) in late January and early February 2026, the DOJ announced that it had identified **over 6 million pages** of potentially responsive documents. Of these, the department released approximately **3.5 million pages** (including about 3 million in the January 30 tranche and prior releases).

Consequently, the number of pages that were **not released** (withheld) is the difference between the total identified and the total released. This amounts to approximately **2.5 million to 3 million pages**. The withheld pages were reportedly comprised of duplicates, non-responsive materials, and documents protected by legal privileges (such as attorney-client privilege or those depicting child sexual abuse material). Several news reports and statements from lawmakers (such as Rep. Ro Khanna) highlighted that "nearly 3 million" or "roughly 50%" of the files remained withheld. Specifically, a report by *China Daily* on February 12, 2026, explicitly stated: "An additional **three million pages** are being withheld, the DOJ said."

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

The breakdown of the 12 tracked vessels is described as follows:
*   **1** aircraft carrier (the **USS Abraham Lincoln**), located in the Arabian Sea off the coast of Oman.
*   **3** Arleigh Burke-class destroyers forming the carrier strike group with the Abraham Lincoln.
*   **2** additional destroyers capable of long-range missile strikes.
*   **3** specialist combat ships positioned at the Bahrain naval station.

*   **1** destroyer in the Red Sea.

Today is February 17, 2026. Following the death of Robert Duvall, many of his iconic roles are being remembered. Which role did Duvall himself often cite as his personal favorite?
(A) Tom Hagen in The Godfather
(B) Augustus McCrae in Lonesome Dove
(C) The evangelical preacher in The Apostle
(D) Boo Radley in To Kill a Mockingbird
(E) The corporate executive in Network
explanation: The article states: "Duvall often said his favourite role was as the Texas Ranger-turned-cowboy Augustus McCrae in Lonesome Dove."
model_output:
Based on Robert Duvall's own frequent statements during his life, the role he cited as his personal favorite was **Augustus "Gus" McCrae** in the 1989 television miniseries ***Lonesome Dove***.

While Duvall is celebrated for his performances as Tom Hagen in *The Godfather* films, the evangelical preacher Euliss "Sonny" Dewey in *The Apostle* (a film he also wrote and directed), and his screen debut as Boo Radley in *To Kill a Mockingbird*, he consistently identified the retired Texas Ranger Gus McCrae as the character he most enjoyed playing. He often spoke of his deep connection to the Western genre and the specific wit and wisdom of the character, calling it "one of the highlights of my life."

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 17, 2026. Economists have noted the decline of Venezuela's state-owned oil company, PDVSA. According to William Jackson of Capital Economics, how does current production compare to levels from 10 to 15 years ago?
(A) It has increased by 300 billion barrels.
(B) It is exactly the same due to private investment.
(C) It has dropped by 211.6 million barrels annually.
(D) It is 1.5 million barrels a day less than it was then.
(E) It has plummeted to zero due to US sanctions.
explanation: Jackson is quoted saying: "Ten to 15 years ago, Venezuela was producing 1.5 million barrels a day more than it does today."
model_output:
Based on reports from **February 2026**, specifically referencing comments by **William Jackson**, chief emerging markets economist at **Capital Economics**, Venezuela's state-owned oil company PDVSA has suffered from years of neglect and underinvestment.

Jackson explicitly stated: "Ten to 15 years ago, Venezuela was producing **1.5 million barrels a day more** than it does today." Consequently, current production levels are 1.5 million barrels per day lower than they were during that previous period.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is February 17, 2026. Jesse Jackson's 1988 speech to the Democratic National Convention is remembered for a specific refrain that was later echoed by Barack Obama. What was that refrain?
(A) "Hope and change"
(B) "Yes we can"
(C) "The rainbow coalition"
(D) "A new ball game"
(E) "Keep hope alive"
explanation: The article states: "His speech to the 1988 Democratic National Convention, which ended with the refrain 'keep hope alive', would be echoed decades later..."
model_output:
Jesse Jackson's 1988 speech to the Democratic National Convention in Atlanta is most famous for its concluding refrain, **"Keep hope alive."** In the speech, Jackson urged Americans to maintain hope in the face of economic and social hardships, famously chanting, "Keep hope alive! Keep hope alive!"

This refrain and the broader theme of "hope" were later echoed by Barack Obama during his 2008 presidential campaign, which utilized the slogans "Hope" (often on the iconic Shepard Fairey poster) and "Change we can believe in," as well as "Yes We Can." While "Yes We Can" is also a famous Obama slogan (adapted from the United Farm Workers' "Sí, se puede"), the specific refrain from Jackson's 1988 speech that is cited as a precursor to Obama's message of hope is "Keep hope alive."

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Jesse Jackson: Veteran civil rights leader who helped reshape America - Nile Post] (https://nilepost.co.ug/world/jesse-jackson-veteran-civil-rights-leader-who-helped-reshape-america-10052026)
- [Jesse Jackson's 1988 Democratic Convention Speech | PBS] (https://www.pbs.org/wgbh/pages/frontline/jesse/speeches/jesse88.html)
- [Keep Hope Alive - Grokipedia] (https://grokipedia.com/encyclopedia/keep-hope-alive/)
- [Jesse Jackson's America First | Compact] (https://compactmag.com/article/jesse-jackson-s-america-first)
